title: Submodules, Nested Repositories, and Dependency Boundaries · History Brief: Gita Submodule Alternative Decision
summary: A product pins three component repositories, one URL moved, a fork rewrites a dependency, recursive checkout executes untrusted configuration, and release builds disagree on nested state. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita submodule alternative decision increment in submodule dependency and trust manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare submodules with package managers, subtree merges, vendoring, monorepos, and build-time fetches by reproducibility, ownership, atomicity, and operations.
Treat URLs, protocols, hooks, recursive commands, credentials, relative paths, and untrusted nested code as explicit trust boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Submodule Alternative Decision
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G86 is a theory in a disposable repository. the release mismatch comes from an uninitialized nested gitlink rather than branch drift. The learner must predict prefixes and object IDs expose initialized, mismatched, and conflicted nested states, run git submodule status --recursive, explain the parser submodule is uninitialized and its recorded gitlink names c0ffee0, test this changed condition: make the referenced dependency commit unreachable remotely and fail the release transfer gate, and preserve this recovery boundary: archive superproject and reachable dependency refs before URL or gitlink migration.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Submodule Alternative Decision contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare submodules with package managers, subtree merges, vendoring, monorepos, and build-time fetches by reproducibility, ownership, atomicity, and operations.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Gita Submodule Alternative Decision while producing submodule dependency and trust manifest.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G86 is a worked example in a disposable repository. the release mismatch comes from an uninitialized nested gitlink rather than branch drift. The learner must predict prefixes and object IDs expose initialized, mismatched, and conflicted nested states, run git submodule status --recursive, explain the parser submodule is uninitialized and its recorded gitlink names c0ffee0, test this changed condition: make the referenced dependency commit unreachable remotely and fail the release transfer gate, and preserve this recovery boundary: archive superproject and reachable dependency refs before URL or gitlink migration. Competency probe: Compare submodules with package managers, subtree merges, vendoring, monorepos, and build-time fetches by reproducibility, ownership, atomicity, and operations. A defensible solution applies Gita Submodule Alternative Decision, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to submodule dependency and trust manifest.

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-submodule-alternative-decision — Compare submodules with package managers, subtree merges, vendoring, monorepos, and build-time fetches by reproducibility, ownership, atomicity, and operations.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 3
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the release mismatch comes from an uninitialized nested gitlink rather than branch drift
git submodule status --recursive
# expected: prefixes and object IDs expose initialized, mismatched, and conflicted nested states
# observation: the parser submodule is uninitialized and its recorded gitlink names c0ffee0
# changed-case: make the referenced dependency commit unreachable remotely and fail the release transfer gate
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: archive superproject and reachable dependency refs before URL or gitlink migration
# transfer-limit: never execute untrusted nested hooks, builds, or recursive commands outside isolation
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Submodules are always obsolete because package managers can represent every source dependency. This fails the Gita Submodule Alternative Decision contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Gita Submodule Alternative Decision evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-submodule-alternative-decision-4cdu15" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Submodule Alternative Decision into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-submodule-alternative-decision-4cdu15
# scenario-key: trace-4cdu15 probe-51udc4 boundary-du154c owner-4cdu15x fault-4cdu15z
# before-ref: main=a11ce4c candidate=b00b115
# hypothesis: the release mismatch comes from an uninitialized nested gitlink rather than branch drift
git submodule status --recursive
# expected: prefixes and object IDs expose initialized, mismatched, and conflicted nested states
# observation: the parser submodule is uninitialized and its recorded gitlink names c0ffee0
# changed-case: make the referenced dependency commit unreachable remotely and fail the release transfer gate
# after-ref: main=a11ce4c candidate=c0ffee5
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: archive superproject and reachable dependency refs before URL or gitlink migration
# transfer-limit: never execute untrusted nested hooks, builds, or recursive commands outside isolation
